volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 1.2.105 - Section 1.2.210)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 1.2.105
sections.original: quin dicant , non est ; merito ut ne dicant , id est .
sections.translation: as to their saying this , that is not in my power ; as to their saying this deservedly , that is in my power .

sections.label: Section 1.2.106
sections.translation: Was this Charmides a friend of yours ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.106b
sections.translation: He both is and he was . That you may believe it to be so , I will tell you a circumstance as a proof . For after this son of his had squandered away his fortune , and he saw himself being reduced to poverty ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.110
sections.translation: and that his daughter was grown up a young woman , and that she who was both her mother and his own wife was dead ; as he himself was about to go hence to Seleueia [*] he committed to my charge the maiden his daughter , and all his property , and that profligate son .

sections.label: Section 1.2.115
sections.original: haec , si mihi inimicus esset , credo haud crederet .
sections.translation: These , I think , he would not have entrusted to me if he had been unfriendly to me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.116
sections.original: Quid tu , adulescentem , quem esse corruptum vides ,
sections.translation: What say you as to the young man , who you see to be thus profligate , and who has been entrusted to your care and confidence ? Why do you not reform him ? Why do you not train him to frugal habits ? It would have been somewhat more just for you to give attention to that matter ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.120
sections.original: si qui probiorem facere posses , non uti
sections.translation: if you could have somehow made him a better man , and not for you yourself to be a party to the same disreputable conduct , and share your dishonour with his disgrace ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.123
sections.translation: What have I done ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.123b
sections.translation: That which a bad man would do .

sections.label: Section 1.2.123c
sections.translation: That is no name of mine .

sections.label: Section 1.2.124
sections.original: Emistin de adulescente has aedes ( quid taces ?)
sections.translation: Have you not bought this house from that young man ? ( A pause .) Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.125
sections.translation: This , where you yourself are now living .

sections.label: Section 1.2.125b
sections.translation: I did buy it , and I gave the money for it ,— forty minae [*] , to the young man himself , into his own hand .

sections.label: Section 1.2.127
sections.translation: You gave the money , do you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.127b
sections.translation: ’ Twas done ; and I am not sorry ’ twas done .

sections.label: Section 1.2.128
sections.translation: I ’ faith — a young man committed to untrusty keeping . Have you not by these means given him a sword with which to slay himself ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.130
sections.original: quid secus est aut quid interest dare te in manus
sections.translation: For , prithee , what else is it , your giving ready money to a young man who loves women , and weak in intellect , with which to complete his edifice of folly which he had already commenced ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.133
sections.translation: Ought I not to have paid him the money ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.133b
sections.translation: You ought not to have paid him ; nor ought you either to have bought anything of or sold anything to him ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.135
sections.original: nec qui deterior esset faceres copiam .
sections.translation: nor should you have provided him with the means of becoming worse . Have you not taken in the person who was entrusted to you ? Have you not driven out of his house the man who entrusted him to you ? By my faith , a pretty trust , and a faithful guardianship ! Leave him to take care of himself ; he would manage his own affairs much better .

sections.label: Section 1.2.140
sections.translation: You overpower me , Megaronides , with your accusations , in a manner so strange , that what was privately entrusted to my secrecy , fidelity , and constancy , for me to tell it to no one , nor make it public , the same I am now compelled to entrust to you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.145
sections.original: Mihi quod credideris sumes ubi posiveris .
sections.translation: Whatever you shall entrust to me , you shall take up the same where you have laid it down .

sections.label: Section 1.2.146
sections.original: Circumspicedum te , ne quis adsit arbiter
sections.translation: Look round you , then , that no overlooker may be near us ( MEGARONIDES looks on every side ) ; and look around every now and then , I beg of you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.148
sections.translation: I am listening if you have aught to say .

sections.label: Section 1.2.148b
sections.translation: If you will be silent , I will speak . At the time when Charmides set out hence for foreign parts ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.150
sections.original: thensaurum demonstravit mihi in hisce aedibus ,
sections.translation: he showed me a treasure in this house , here in a certain closet — ( He starts as if he hears a noise .) But do look around .

sections.label: Section 1.2.152
sections.translation: There is no one .

sections.label: Section 1.2.152b
sections.translation: Of Philippean pieces [*] to the number of three thousand . Alone with myself , in tears , he entreated me , by our friendship and by my honour , not to entrust this to his son , nor yet to any one , from whom that might come to his knowledge .

sections.label: Section 1.2.155
sections.original: neu quoiquam unde ad eum id posset permanascere .
sections.translation: Now , if he comes back hither safe , I will restore to him his own . But if anything should happen to him , at all events I have a stock from which to give a marriage-portion to his daughter , who has been entrusted to me , that I may settle her in a condition of life that befits her .

sections.label: Section 1.2.160
sections.original: Pro di immortales , verbis paucis quam cito
sections.translation: O ye immortal gods ! how soon , in a few words , you have made another man of me ; I came to you quite a different person . But , as you have begun , proceed further to inform me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.163
sections.original: Quid tibi ego dicam , qui illius sapientiam
sections.translation: What shall I tell you ? How that this worthless fellow had almost utterly ruined his caution and my own trustiness and all the secret .

sections.label: Section 1.2.166
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.166b
sections.original: Quia , ruri dum sum ego unos sex dies ,
sections.translation: Because , while I was in the country for only six days , in my absence and without my knowledge , without consulting me , he advertised with bills [*] this house for sale .

sections.label: Section 1.2.169
sections.translation: The wolf hungered the more , and opened his mouth the wider ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.170
sections.translation: he watched till [*] the dog went to sleep ; and intended to carry off the whole entire flock .

sections.label: Section 1.2.172
sections.original: Fecisset edepol , ni haec praesensisset canes .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he would have done it , if the dogs had not perceived this in time . But now , in my turn , I wish to ask you this : let me know what it was my duty for me to do .

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: utrum indicare me ei thensaurum aequom fuit ,
sections.translation: Whether was it right for me to discover the treasure to him , against which very thing his father had cautioned me , or should I have permitted another person to become the owner of this house ? Ought that money to have belonged to him who bought the house ? In preference , I myself bought the house ; I gave the money

sections.label: Section 1.2.180
sections.original: thensauri causa , ut salvom amico traderem .
sections.translation: for the sake of the treasure , that I might deliver it safe to my friend . I have not , then , bought this house either for myself or for my own use ; for Charmides have I bought it back again ; from my own store have I paid the money . This , whether it has been done rightfully or wrongfully , I own , Megaronides , that I have done .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185
sections.original: em mea malefacta , em meam aváritiam tibi ;
sections.translation: Here , then , are my misdeeds ; here , then , is my avarice . Is it for these things that they spread false reports against me ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.187
sections.translation: Stay — you have overcome your corrector . You have tied my tongue ; there is nothing for me to say in answer .

sections.label: Section 1.2.189
sections.original: Nunc ego te quaeso ut me opera et consilio iuves
sections.translation: Now I entreat you to aid me with your assistance and counsel ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.190
sections.translation: and to share this duty of mine in common with me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.191
sections.translation: I promise you my assistance .

sections.label: Section 1.2.191b
sections.translation: Where , then , will you be a short time hence ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.191c
sections.translation: At home .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192
sections.translation: Do you wish anything else ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.192b
sections.translation: Attend to the trust reposed in you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192c
sections.translation: That is being carefully done .

sections.label: Section 1.2.193
sections.translation: But how say you —?

sections.label: Section 1.2.193b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.193c
sections.translation: Where is the young man living now ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.194
sections.original: Posticulum hoc recepit , quom aedis vendidit .
sections.translation: This back part [*] of the building he retained when he sold the house .

sections.label: Section 1.2.195
sections.original: Istuc volebam scire . i sane nunciam .
sections.translation: That I wanted to know . Now , then , go at once . But what say you , where is the damsel now ? She is at your house , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.196b
sections.translation: She is so ; I take care of her almost as much as of my own daughter .

sections.label: Section 1.2.197b
sections.translation: You act properly .

sections.label: Section 1.2.198
sections.original: Numquid , prius quam abeo , me rogaturu'srogaturus es ?—
sections.translation: Before I go away , are you going to ask me anything else ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.198b
sections.translation: Farewell . ( Exit CALLICLES .) Really , there is nothing more foolish or more stupid ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.200
sections.translation: nothing more lying or indeed more tattling , more self-conceited or more forsworn , than those men of this city everlastingly gossiping about , whom they call Busybodies [*] . And thus have I enlisted myself in their ranks together with them ; who have been the swallower of the false tales

sections.label: Section 1.2.205
sections.original: qui omnia se simulant scire neque quicquam sciunt .
sections.translation: of those who pretend that they know everything , and yet know nothing . They know , forsooth , what each person either has in his mind , or is likely to have ; they know what the king whispered in the ear of the queen ; they know what Juno talked about in conversation with Jupiter ; that which neither is nor is likely to be , do these fellows know .

sections.label: Section 1.2.210
sections.original: falson an vero laudent , culpent quem velint ,
sections.translation: Whether they praise or dispraise any one they please , falsely or truly , they care not a straw , so they know that which they choose to know . All people were in the habit of saying that this Callicles was unworthy of this state , and , himself , to exist , who had despoiled this young man of his property .
